name: Marine Basal Medium + 3 mM ammonium + phytoplankton metabolite (12 mM carbon) (PMC12202767)
description: Marine Basal Medium + 3 mM ammonium + phytoplankton metabolite (12 mM carbon) — medium reported in PMC12202767 (complex/undefined base — extracts not BiGG-mappable).

provenance.citation: Medium composition from PMC12202767 (see GrowthDB record).
provenance.notes: Complex medium: chemically-defined portion mapped to BiGG; biological extracts listed in `unmapped`. Added via GrowthDB curation.
provenance.verification: paper-verified (complete recipe; cited + supplementary sources chased)
provenance.verification_evidence: The MM composition described by Baumann et al. [17] for 1 L of the medium with a pH of 7.5 was 11.7 g of NaCl, 12.32 g of MgSO4.7H2O, 0.745 g of KCl, 1.47 g of CaCl2.2H2O, 6.05 g of Tris (hydroxymethyl) aminomethane (C4H11NO3), 6.65 g of NH4Cl, 0.062 g of K2HPO4.3H2O, and 0.026 g of FeSO4.7H2O.
provenance.base_medium: Baumann marine basal medium (BM), ref 42 = Baumann P. & Baumann L. 1981, 'The marine gram-negative eubacteria: genera Photobacterium, Beneckea, Alteromonas, Pseudomonas, and Alcaligenes', in The Prokaryotes (Springer); recipe originally from Baumann et al. 1971 J. Bacteriol. 107:268-294.

sources_chased.what: base recipe cited as ref 42 (Baumann & Baumann 1981, The Prokaryotes; = Baumann et al. 1971 basal medium)
sources_chased.how: WebSearch + WebFetch of PMC5615306 (Lopez-Cortes et al.), which quotes the Baumann per-liter recipe verbatim
Read corpus JATS XML (Substrate growth tests section)
added: phytoplankton metabolite at 12 mM carbon (C source)

sources_chased.what: whether Baumann BM contains a separate trace-metal solution or vitamins (Co/Cu/Mn/Zn)
sources_chased.how: WebSearch - no evidence links a Co/Cu/Mn/Zn trace-metal mix or vitamins to the cited Baumann basal medium; FeSO4 is its only micronutrient

missing_before: Tris-HCl buffer (6.05 g/L, ~49.9 mM) - the pH 7.5 buffer of the basal medium, absent from current_components
Carbon source (phytoplankton metabolite at 12 mM carbon) - previously only listed as uncovered
Specific salt identities and concentrations for the bulk ions (NaCl, MgSO4.7H2O, KCl, CaCl2.2H2O, K2HPO4.3H2O, FeSO4.7H2O), which were present only as concentration-less exchange placeholders
recuration.notes: Medium = Baumann marine basal medium (8-component defined mineral base) with the paper's modifications: nitrogen supplied as 3 mM ammonium (not the 124 mM NH4Cl of the original Baumann recipe) and carbon supplied as one phytoplankton metabolite at 12 mM C. FeSO4 is the ONLY micronutrient in the cited Baumann BM; there is no separate trace-metal solution and no vitamins. The prior record's Co2+, Cu2+, Mn2+, Zn2+ exchanges and the generic 'Trace metal solution' are NOT supported by the cited Baumann medium and were dropped as unfounded (the 'Fe3+' and 'Fe2+ mitochondria' generic exchanges likewi

components.name: NaCl
components.evidence_source: cited:Baumann (ref 42, Baumann & Baumann 1981 The Prokaryotes; identical basal recipe from Baumann et al. 1971 quoted verbatim in Lopez-Cortes et al. 2010, PMC5615306)

components.name: NaCl
components.evidence_source: cited:Baumann (ref 42, Baumann & Baumann 1981 The Prokaryotes; identical basal recipe from Baumann et al. 1971 quoted verbatim in Lopez-Cortes et al. 2010, PMC5615306)

uncovered.name: Phytoplankton metabolite as sole carbon source
uncovered.note: no exchange auto-assigned; propose uptake -1.0 pending manual curation

oxygen_note: no oxygen requirement specified; O2 available but not asserted
